revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:葦稲葉神社]] {{Expand Japanese|葦稲葉神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ashiinaba Shrine | native_name = 葦稲葉神社 | image = Ashiinaba shrine, Tokushima.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ashiinaba Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.130276|134.412463}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ashiinaba Shrine'''|葦稲葉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|lt=kokushi genzaisha|en|Kokushi genzaisha|fr|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|simple|Kokushi genzaisha|qid=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}} * {{ill|gōshi|lt=gōshi|ja|合祀|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11657514|ltq=Q11657514|qid=Q11657514|1=List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Q723417|ltq=Q723417|qid=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ushihiko Shrine|lt=Ushihiko Shrine|qid=Q135041302|1=Ushihiko Shrine (Q135041302)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1350072|ltq=Q1350072|qid=Q1350072|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}} * {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|lt=Awa Province|be|Ава (правінцыя, Сікоку)|cs|Provincie Awa (Tokušima)|de|Provinz Awa (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|es|Provincia de Awa (Tokushima)|fa|ولایت آوا|fr|Province d'Awa (Tokushima)|gl|Provincia de Awa, Tokushima|id|Provinsi Awa (Tokushima)|it|Awa (provincia, Tokushima)|ja|阿波国|ko|아와국 (난카이도)|lt|Avos žemė (Tokušima)|nl|Awa (Tokushima)|pt|Província de Awa (Tokushima)|ru|Ава (Сикоку)|simple|Awa Province (Tokushima)|sk|Awa (provincia, Tokušima)|sr|Провинција Ава (Шикоку)|tr|Awa (vilayet, Tokushima)|uk|Провінція Ава (Шікоку)|zh_min_nan|Awa-kok (Nankaidô)|zh|阿波國|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} * {{ill|Q1156025|ltq=Q1156025|qid=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.130276|134.412463}} == image (P18) == * Ashiinaba shrine, Tokushima.jpg == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140301224331/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/540201.html * {{ill|Q611399|ltq=Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183176 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|lt=nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|fr|Nagare-zukuri|it|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|pl|Nagare-zukuri|ru|Нагарэ-дзукури|sv|Nagare-zukuri|zh|流造|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あしいなばじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6480005003721 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135041301|ltq=Q135041301|qid=Q135041301|1=Kaehimeno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県板野郡上板町神宅字宮ノ北45', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:葦稲葉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11621139}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ashiinaba Shrine |Image = [[File:Ashiinaba shrine, Tokushima.jpg|270px|Ashiinaba Shrine]] |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|qid=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|qid=Q1350072|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}} Jintaku Aza Miyanokita 45 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ashiinaba-no-Kami |Shrine rank = Shrine listed in the Six National Histories
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Date of foundation unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Tonomiya |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 22 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Ashiinaba Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ashiinaba Shrine''' (あしいなばじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|qid=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|qid=Q1350072|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Ashiinaba-no-Kami''' ({{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}) is enshrined as the principal deity, with '''Kagaehime-no-Mikoto''' ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanohime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) {{ill|lt=enshrined together|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}. === History === Two main halls stand side by side; the western one (on the left when facing them) is Ashiinaba Shrine (the eastern one is the {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} Tonomiya Shrine). Although the date of foundation is unknown, the shrine has been worshipped since ancient times. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|qid=Q24730|1=842}}), it was promoted from having no rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade“{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}},” entry for the 2nd day of the 10th month, 9th year of Jōwa.. It was later promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}) it was further elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},” entry for the 23rd day of the 4th month, 9th year of Jōgan.. In the 16th year of the same era it was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower GradeSame source, entry for the 14th day of the 3rd month, 16th year of Jōgan., and in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=879|Q25001|qid=Q25001|1=879}}) to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper GradeSame source, entry for the 23rd day of the 6th month, 3rd year of Gangyō.. It is thus recognized as a {{ill|lt=shrine recorded in the national histories|Q118304363|qid=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}}. The jointly enshrined Kagaehime-no-Mikoto is identified with the small shrine “Kagaehime Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|qid=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=candidate for an Engishiki shrine|Q135009906|qid=Q135009906|1=Massha}}There is also a theory that identifies it with a small shrine within the grounds of {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|qid=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|qid=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}, Ōasa Town.. The time and circumstances under which it was enshrined together are completely unknown, but in this area stands the low yet beautifully shaped {{ill|lt=Mount Ōyama (Kagawa and Tokushima)|Q11434564|qid=Q11434564|lt=Mount Ōyama|1=Mount Ōyama}}. As the shrine is located almost at the center of the {{ill|lt=alluvial fan|Q465088|qid=Q465088|1=Alluvial fan}} on its southern foothills, it is conjectured that the mountain itself was revered as a deity and that both deities were enshrined as {{ill|lt=guardian deities|Q131278957|qid=Q131278957|1=Guardian deities}} of the rice fields spreading across the foothillsAkiyama, “Kagaehime Shrine.”. According to shrine tradition, in ancient times the shrine was located in a reed field called Ashinohara in Ōyamahata on the mid-slopes of Mount ŌyamaNearby is {{ill|lt=Ōyama-ji (Kamiita, Tokushima)|Q3514063|qid=Q3514063|lt=Ōyama-ji|1=Taisan-ji (Kamiita)}}, said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}.. Due to flooding it was moved to Miyagadani, and later, after suffering arson, it was relocated to its present site on the southern foothills. However, a {{ill|lt=bronze bell|Q2312868|qid=Q2312868|1=Dōtaku}} was also excavated near the present site in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|qid=Q2084|1=1915}}), suggesting that a large settlement had already developed in this area as early as the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|qid=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}}. Furthermore, numerous {{ill|lt=tumuli|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} are distributed around the foothills of Mount Ōyama, indicating that development progressed from the Yayoi period onward. During the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tonomiya Shrine – Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and stands as a subsidiary shrine of the same scale immediately east of the main shrine’s main hall. The alternative name “Tonomiya” of this shrine refers to this. In addition, there are several {{ill|lt=branch shrines|Q135009906|qid=Q135009906|1=Massha}} nearby, including Shingū Shrine, Tsurugi Shrine, and Yamagami Shrine. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *Akiyama Yasushi, “Kagaehime Shrine” (in *Survey Reports on Engishiki Shrines*, Vol. 23, Nankaidō, published by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University Press|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press}}, 1987) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ashiinaba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ashiinaba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:徳島県の旧村社]] [[Category:上板町の建築物]] [[Category:上板町の歴史]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]]
